title: Binary size threshold and automatic promotion to refc
purpose: With ProcBin available (B-072), the system needs a decision point: when should a binary be stored inline vs. off-heap? BEAM uses 64 bytes as the threshold — binaries <= 64 bytes are heap binaries, > 64 bytes become refc binaries. This brief implements that threshold at every binary creation point: `write_binary()`, BinaryBuilder finalization, and BIF results that produce binaries.
task: Add a configurable size threshold. Modify all binary creation paths to check the threshold and route to either inline `write_binary()` or `write_proc_bin()`.

requirements.title: Configurable threshold constant
requirements.spec: Define the binary size threshold as a configurable constant.
acceptance: `const REFC_BINARY_THRESHOLD: usize = 64` defined in term/binary.rs or term/shared_binary.rs
Binaries with `len <= REFC_BINARY_THRESHOLD` are stored inline
Binaries with `len > REFC_BINARY_THRESHOLD` are stored as ProcBin
The threshold is documented

requirements.title: Smart binary allocation function
requirements.spec: Create a single entry point that decides inline vs. refc based on the threshold.
acceptance: `alloc_binary(heap, bytes) -> Option<Term>` checks byte length against threshold
If `<= threshold`: calls existing `write_binary(heap, bytes)` (inline)
If `> threshold`: creates SharedBinary, calls `write_proc_bin(heap, shared)` (refc)
Returns the same `Term` type regardless of storage strategy
All callers get transparent inline/refc selection
Test: 64-byte binary → inline (Binary tag 0x16)
Test: 65-byte binary → refc (ProcBin tag 0x19)
Test: 0-byte binary → inline

requirements.title: BinaryBuilder finalization uses threshold
requirements.spec: Modify `finalize_builder()` to route through `alloc_binary()` so large constructed binaries automatically become refc.
acceptance: BinaryBuilder producing <= 64 bytes finalizes to inline Binary
BinaryBuilder producing > 64 bytes finalizes to ProcBin
Existing bs_init/bs_put/finalize pipeline works unchanged
Test: build a 100-byte binary via bs_put instructions → result is ProcBin

requirements.title: BIF binary results use threshold
requirements.spec: Modify BIF helpers that produce binary results (make_leaked_binary, iodata_append, string operations) to use the threshold.
acceptance: BIF results that produce binaries > 64 bytes automatically become ProcBin
Small BIF results remain inline
Existing BIF tests continue to pass
Test: `iodata_append` producing > 64 bytes returns ProcBin

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the threshold at runtime — it is set at VM creation and fixed
SHALL NOT modify the inline Binary format — small binaries are unchanged
SHALL NOT implement sub-binary promotion — that's B-074
The default threshold SHALL be 64 bytes (matching BEAM)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
